title: EU Parliament greenlights Chat Control 1.0
titleZh: 欧洲议会批准“聊天控制1.0”
author: rapnie
relevanceReason: 命中 1 个 AI/科技关键词。
summaryRaw: Today, the European Parliament allowed the suspicionless mass scanning of private communications (“Chat Control 1.0”) to pass, a measure it had rejected twice in March. Although a majority of voting Members of the European Parliament (MEPs) actually opposed the regulation (314 against, 276 in favor, 17 abstentions), the motion to reject it failed to secure the required absolute majority of 361 votes. As a result, mass scanning is now permitted again until 2028.

A symbolic exemption was adopted for encrypted communications—though in practice, service providers do not scan these anyway. Furthermore, while a majority of voting MEPs wanted to restrict the scanning of private communications strictly to suspects identified by the judiciary (322 to 255 votes), this amendment likewise fell short of the required absolute majority.
Dr. Patrick Breyer, civil rights activist and former Member of the European Parliament (MEP), warns of the consequences:

“The fact that Chat Control is moving forward against the will of the majority of voting MEPs is a farce and damages democracy. Our children are the real losers in this undemocratic process. The passage of a genuine, permanent child protection regulation is now in serious jeopardy. The Council will never agree to a desperately needed paradigm shift as long as they can simply stick to the old approach of suspicionless scanning at the whim of the tech industry.”
Despite the legislative defeat, Breyer remains defiant regarding the upcoming negotiations:
summaryZh: Today, the European Parliament allowed the suspicionless mass scanning of private communications (“Chat Control 1.0”) to pass, a measure it had rejected twice in March. Although a majority of voting Members of the European Parliament (MEPs) actually opposed the regulation (314 against, 276 in favor, 17 abstentions), the motion to reject it failed to secure the required absolute majority of 361 votes. As a result, mass scanning is now permitted again until 2028.

A symbolic exemption was adopted for encrypted communications—though in practice, service providers do not scan these anyway. Furthermore, while a majority of voting MEPs wanted to restrict the scanning of private communications strictly to suspects identified by the judiciary (322 to 255 votes), this amendment likewise fell short of the required absolute majority.

comments.author: fosk
comments.textRawHtml:
The EU is a farce, an undemocratic virtue signaling organization, and this is why:

- The Parliament voted against the first reading of this proposal twice in 2026, the first time they only supported limited cases for it, while the second time they actually defeated it fully.

- The Commission didn't care, and kept the proposal on the table by refusing to withdraw it.

- Once the Commission does that, the proposal goes on second-reading (despite the first-reading having defeated it) and it is established in a very PERVERSE way in EU law that to AVOID passing the proposal in second-reading you need ABSOLUTE majority which is incredibly hard to pursue (you would think that we would need an absolute majority to PASS a proposal that was previously defeated on first-reading, not instead needing absolute majority to DENY a previously defeated proposal that was again forced to the table).

- Furthermore, absences in practicality count as "No" on the rejection. So of course they scheduled the vote in the summer when notoriously there will be many absences.

By never withdrawing a defeated proposal they can effectively and in practicality pursue any agenda they want (it requires a massive mobilization effort to find absolute majority to defeat any proposal, especially when absences for any reason effectively count against rejection).

In PRACTICALITY, the Commission can pursue any agenda whenever and however they want, and throw the votes down the drain.

EU's democracy is lipstick on a pig.
comments.textZhHtml:
欧盟是一场闹剧，一个不民主的美德信号组织，这就是为什么：

- 议会在 2026 年两次对该提案的一读投了反对票，第一次他们只支持了有限的案例，而第二次他们实际上完全否决了它。

- 欧盟委员会并不在意，并拒绝撤回该提案，从而将其保留在桌面上。

- 一旦欧盟委员会这样做了也就是说，该提案将进行二读（尽管一读击败了它），并且欧盟法律以一种非常反常的方式规定，为了避免在二读中通过该提案，您需要获得绝对多数，而这很难实现（您可能认为我们需要绝对多数来通过先前在一读中被击败的提案，而不是需要绝对多数来拒绝先前被击败的提案，该提案再次被迫通过表）。

- 此外，不具备实用性将被视为拒绝。因此，他们当然将投票安排在夏季，众所周知，届时会有很多人缺席。

通过从不撤回失败的提案，他们可以有效地、在实践中追求他们想要的任何议程（需要大规模的动员努力，找到绝对多数来击败任何提案，特别是当因任何原因缺席实际上不会被拒绝时）。

在实践中，委员会可以随时随地追求任何议程，并将选票投给委员会流失。

欧盟的民主是猪身上的口红。

children.author: flossly
children.textRawHtml:
It's worse than a farce. It's actually evil. The way "they" are pushing chat control, and how Ursula is avoiding justice (she has a huge corruption case hanging over her head but manages continuously cancel court dates); this makes a all too clear.

The only way to fix this is EU member states become more critical: but for politicians in those states an EU position is the best promotion they can make. So almost nobody dares to speak out.
children.textZhHtml:
这比一场闹剧还要糟糕。其实是邪恶的。 “他们”推动聊天控制的方式，以及乌苏拉如何逃避正义（她有一个巨大的腐败案件悬而未决，但设法不断取消法庭日期）；这一切都太清楚了。

解决这个问题的唯一方法是欧盟成员国变得更加挑剔：但对于这些国家的政治家来说，欧盟的立场是他们能做出的最好的晋升。所以几乎没人敢发声。

children.author: gozucito
children.textRawHtml:
Could you please give more detail about this for those of us less informed of EU things? What corrupt things has Ursula done? Or what has she been accused of, exactly? How long has she been cancelling court dates?

Who is the engineer of all this evil? Her? Someone else?
children.textZhHtml:
您能为我们这些对欧盟情况不太了解的人提供更多详细信息吗？乌苏拉做了什么腐败的事？或者说她到底被指控了什么？她取消出庭日期已经多久了？

谁是这一切罪恶的策划者？她？还有其他人吗？

children.author: hk__2
children.textRawHtml: Her name is von der Leyen. "Ursula" is fine for your friends, but it’s awkward for a public figure. Do you write "Donald" as well?
children.textZhHtml: 她的名字是冯德莱恩。 “乌苏拉”对你的朋友来说还好，但对公众人物来说就很尴尬了。你也写“唐纳德”吗？

children.author: barney54
children.textRawHtml: I write Donald sometimes.  If you say Donald in the right context we know who are talking about.  Or Kier or Emmanuel. There is nothing wrong with first names of public figures.
children.textZhHtml: 我有时给唐纳德写信。  如果您在正确的上下文中说唐纳德，我们就知道在谈论谁。  或者基尔或伊曼纽尔。公众人物的名字没有任何问题。

children.author: root-parent
children.textRawHtml: You have 3 million threads here to police, talking about Elon. Get on with it...
children.textZhHtml: 这里有 300 万个帖子可供监管，谈论 Elon。继续吧...

children.author: Fnoord
children.textRawHtml: Because in the name Elon Musk, Elon stands out.
children.textZhHtml: 因为埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 这个名字，埃隆 (Elon Musk) 脱颖而出。

children.author: philipallstar
children.textRawHtml: The "Awkward Police" is not a job.

children.author: nancyminusone
children.textRawHtml: yeah, I do write 'Donald', as do a lot of people. Awkward only if you are talking about the duck or the hamburger clown.
children.textZhHtml: 是的，我确实写了“唐纳德”，就像很多人一样。只有当你谈论鸭子或汉堡小丑时才显得尴尬。

children.author: boltzmann-brain
children.textRawHtml: It's very usual to name famous people by their first name. like John, Ringo, George, or Paul. Or Steve and Bill. Or Sam and Dario.
children.textZhHtml: 以名字命名名人是很常见的。像约翰、林戈、乔治或保罗。或者史蒂夫和比尔。或者山姆和达里奥。

children.author: stronglikedan
children.textRawHtml: what? it's her name. people use it. it's fine. you act like politicians deserve respect
children.textZhHtml: 什么？这是她的名字。人们使用它。没关系。你表现得像政客一样值得尊重

children.author: pfortuny
children.textRawHtml:
That summurizes it pretty well. The Commission is essentially omnipotent, and there is no relevat judiciary to stop it.

This is farcical.

Yes, from a PURELY MONETARY viewpoint, Brexit was a disaster. But there are more important things than money.

I'm from Spain btw.
children.textZhHtml:
这很好地总结了这一点。委员会本质上是无所不能的，并且没有相关的司法机构来阻止它。

这是荒谬的。

是的，从纯粹的货币角度来看，英国脱欧是一场灾难。但有比金钱更重要的东西。

顺便说一句，我来自西班牙。

children.author: rayiner
children.textRawHtml: I tried to explain to my wife (American lawyer) how the EU Commissioners are selected and she told me "I'm not having fun understanding this." I had thought it was just the heads of government from each member state. That would make sense given the immense power of the body. But that's apparently not how it works.
children.textZhHtml: 我试图向我的妻子（美国律师）解释欧盟委员是如何选出的，她告诉我“我对理解这一点并不感兴趣。”我原以为只是各个成员国的政府首脑。考虑到身体的巨大力量，这是有道理的。但这显然不是它的工作原理。

children.author: ktosobcy
children.textRawHtml:
You are mixing it with Council ;)

Yeah, EU setup is absurdly convoluted but it's simply a result of the consensus that tires to balance difference sources of power (the people and the countries).

Ideally the whole EU population should just vote for MEPs in EU parliment, which the would form the government. But that would completely sidestep member states government which they won't ever agree to. And any even mention of reforminng the EU towards that is immediatelly shoot down because nationalists are screetching about soverignty (which at the same time complain that the EU is undemocratic)

So we have the setup with European Council (heads of governments) setting the agenda, Comission (presiden of which is selected by council and approved by parliment) that makes that into law and then Parliment and EU Council (not European council!) votes for it.

A coupld of infographics how that works:

https://www.europarl.europa.eu/topics/en/article/20240524STO...

https://static.cfr.org/sites/default/files/image/2022/03/-la...

https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/wp-content/uploads/2022...
children.textZhHtml:
你把它与理事会混为一谈了;)

是的，欧盟的设置错综复杂，但这只是共识的结果，疲于平衡不同的权力来源（人民和国家）。

理想情况下，全体欧盟人口应该投票支持欧盟议会中的欧洲议会议员，他们将组成政府。但这将完全回避成员国政府，而他们永远不会同意。甚至任何有关改革欧盟的提及都会立即被否决，因为民族主义者正在大声疾呼主权（同时抱怨欧盟不民主）

因此，我们与欧洲理事会（政府首脑）制定议程，委员会（其主席由理事会选出并经议会批准）将其纳入法律，然后议会和欧盟理事会（不是欧洲理事会！）投票通过

一些信息图表说明其工作原理：

https://www.europarl.europa.eu/topics/en/article/20240524STO...

https://static.cfr.org/sites/default/files/image/2022/03/-la...

https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/wp-content/uploads/2022...

children.author: Fnoord
children.textRawHtml:
Yup, classic, mixed EP with EC. Makes you wonder if he explained it correctly to his wife (American lawyer), or if she verified.

I don't like the way US presidents are elected either, ignoring popular vote, and including gerrymandering. I also don't like how the US president is allowed to bypass Congress.
children.textZhHtml:
是的，经典，混合 EP 与 EC。让你怀疑他向他的妻子（美国律师）解释的是否正确，或者她是否证实了。

我也不喜欢美国总统的选举方式，无视普选，包括不公正地划分选区。我也不喜欢美国总统绕过国会。

children.author: rayiner
children.textRawHtml:
> he explained it correctly to his wife (American lawyer), or if she verified.

Are you saying I am mixing it up now? My post acknowledged I had mixed it up before: given that the Commission is so powerful, I had thought it was comprised of the heads of state. I’m aware that’s not how it’s structured. I was explaining it to my wife because the reality is very complicated!

> I don't like the way US presidents are elected either, ignoring popular vote and including gerrymandering.

Gerrymandering cannot affect a Presidential election, because the Electoral College is elected based on statewide popular votes. And the Electoral College is somewhere in-between direct election of the chief executive, as in France, and indirect election of the chief executive, as in most european countries.

In every case where the U.S. electoral college vote produced a different result than the national popular vote, the President’s party still won the Congressional popular vote. So the outcome would have been the same if our rules were like most of Europe, where the winning party picks the chief executive.
children.textZhHtml:
> 他向他的妻子（美国律师）正确解释了这一点，或者她是否证实了。

你是说我现在混淆了吗？我的帖子承认我之前把它搞混了：鉴于委员会如此强大，我以为它是由国家元首组成的。我知道它的结构不是这样的。我正在向我的妻子解释，因为现实很复杂！

>我也不喜欢美国总统的选举方式，无视普选，还包括不公正地划分选区。

不公正地划分选区不会影响总统选举，因为选举团是根据全州普选票选出的。选举团制度介于行政长官直接选举（如法国）和行政长官间接选举（如大多数欧洲国家）之间。

在美国选举团投票产生与全国普选结果不同的情况下，总统所在的政党仍然赢得了国会普选。因此，如果我们的规则像欧洲大多数国家一样，由获胜政党选出首席执行官，结果也会是一样的。
